title: Stars and Stories: Piglet's Birthday Surprise
summary: Piglet wants to make Eeyore's birthday special by giving him a bright red balloon. After getting it inflated by Christopher Robin, Piglet accidentally trips and pops the gift. Undeterred, he returns home to find a special gold balloon that is resistant to popping. When he finds Christopher Robin is out fishing, Piglet and Winnie the Pooh wait patiently for his return. Once the gold balloon is finally inflated, Piglet carefully delivers it to Eeyore at the stream, giving the donkey a wonderful surprise that he ties to his tail to enjoy all day long.

highlightQuote.text: The gold one... it doesn't pop very well. It doesn't pop.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dad, would you like a little something?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Excuse me... Okay, like a little something like what?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Maybe a blanket?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: A blanket? Okay. Thank you. Would you like a blanket? Okay, thank you. Do you want to snuggle up in my lap while we tell stories?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I'm going to put this over your lap.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, thank you. And you're going to climb right in?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah. Nope.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh okay. I'll just snuggle up with my blanket. Thank you.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I'm not going to have a blanket. I'm going to sit here and watch.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, okay. Okay, and now it's time for stars and stories. Hmm, who should tonight's story be about?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It's going to be about Piglet with the red balloon and Eeyore. This is his birthday story.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, okay. Well, once upon a time, Piglet happened to know that today was Eeyore's birthday. And so what did Piglet do?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: He tried to go up to his house with Eeyore's balloon.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And what did he see?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: He saw a red balloon and then he saw a packet full of balloons and they were green balloons and blue balloons and pink, green and even purple balloons and even red balloons and even all the other colors.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Piglet said to himself, "Gosh, I think Eeyore might like one of these for his birthday. I think I'll pick one." And what did he pick?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: He picked his favorite color: red.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: He picked a red balloon. And was the balloon already inflated big or was it not inflated yet?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It's not inflated yet.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, so Piglet said, "Gosh, I have a problem. I have to blow up this balloon for Eeyore, but I'm too little to blow it up on my own. What can I do?"

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Christopher Robin's at your house too.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, I'll just go find Christopher Robin, maybe he can help. So Piglet took the red balloon that was not inflated yet and he walked over to Christopher Robin's house and he knocked on the door. And then what happened?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And Christopher Robin answered the door.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And he said, "Oh, hello Piglet. What can I do for you today?" And what did Piglet say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: This balloon is not inflated yet.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, it's not inflated yet, said Christopher Robin. I see. And would you like me to blow it up for you, Piglet?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, I would happily do that. And so Christopher Robin took the beautiful red balloon and put it to his lips and blew into it, the balloon got bigger and bigger. And then he pinched it shut and said, "There, that's almost as big as you, Piglet. Is that a good size?"

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And then I tied it.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Exactly, and then he tied it so that it would stay inflated. And he said, "Well, there you are, Piglet."

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And soon he ran to Eeyore and he fell down flat on his face.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, Piglet was running and fell down flat on his face, and so what happened?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: He tripped. Yeah.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yeah. And what happened to the balloon?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I don't know.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: The balloon went bang and it popped underneath Piglet. And Piglet was very surprised because he didn't know that when he tripped and fell that he would pop the balloon by accident.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And Piglet, you got some more balloons at your house, Piglet.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Piglet thought to himself, "Gosh, I have more balloons in my house. And since it's Eeyore's birthday, maybe I could just go get him another one." And what did he decide to do?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: The gold one... it doesn't pop very well. It doesn't pop.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, the gold one doesn't pop. Ah, yes. And so Piglet went back to his house and got a gold balloon. But he needed to get it inflated. So what did he do this time?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: This time he needed Christopher Robin again. And then he threw his red balloon in the trash.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well yeah, the red balloon had popped, so he threw it away. And he took the gold balloon and he went to Christopher Robin's house. But when he got there, there was a sign on the door and it said "Gone Fishin'". And so Piglet said, "Hmm, I guess Christopher Robin is not here. I need to find some other way to blow up the balloon."

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Maybe Pooh can do that.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh yeah. And so Piglet went to Pooh Bear's house and knocked on Pooh Bear's little door. And then what happened?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Pooh answered it.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And what did Winnie the Pooh say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I'm too small to blow up the balloon for you, Piglet.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Piglet said, "Oh no, but it's Eeyore's birthday. What are we going to do?"

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Do waiting if you want to. Piglet, waiting is a good idea.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, waiting is a good idea. But Piglet said, "But how will that blow up the balloon?"

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: You can wait. You can wait.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Hmm. And Pooh Bear said, "Perhaps if we wait and sit and think on it a bit, we'll have an idea." And so Winnie the Pooh and Piglet sat down with their gold balloon and waited and thought for a bit. And then along came another one of their friends. Who came along?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Christopher Robin.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Christopher Robin came back from his fishing trip. He had a fishing rod and a bucket full of fish that he had caught and he said, "Oh hello Piglet. Hello Pooh Bear. Piglet, what happened to that balloon that I blew up for you?"

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It got threw in the trash because I popped it by accident.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, that's a shame, Piglet. Sorry to hear that. Oh, what's that you got there? Is that a gold balloon? Maybe you'd like me to blow that one up for you.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes, and it said it doesn't pop.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, it's one of those balloons that doesn't pop. I think Eeyore would like that very much for his birthday. Have you thought about giving him a balloon for a present?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And remember to walk slowly, Piglet.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yes, that's good advice. And so Pooh Bear said, "Well look at that, my idea worked. We just waited around and then Christopher Robin showed up." And meanwhile, Christopher Robin had picked up the gold balloon and was blowing it up. And it got bigger and bigger and bigger and then he said, "Okay, now I'll tie it up." And Piglet said, "That looks perfect." And then what did Piglet do?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Daaaaaddy... you know keep going the story.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh yeah. So then, Piglet took the golden balloon and he rushed over to find Eeyore. And where did he find Eeyore?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: At the stream.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh yes, of course. Eeyore was at the stream looking at his reflection, muttering to himself about how "Oh, it's my birthday, but no one has given me any presents at all."

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: But, but Piglet got a present for you.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And so Piglet runs up with the gold balloon and says "But I've got a present for you, Eeyore." And Eeyore says "You got me a what, Piglet? Did you... is that a present for me?" And what does Piglet say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It is.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And what is it?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: A balloon.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: A balloon. My favorite. I love balloons, says Eeyore.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And then Piglet gave Eeyore the golden balloon and Eeyore was so very happy to have a beautiful gold balloon that he tied it right onto his tail so that it floated up above him in the sky all day as he went about his business in the forest. And Piglet saw the balloon drifting about behind Eeyore as he sang to himself all day and Piglet was very happy with the gift. The end.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Another story about Piglet with another balloon?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, we told our one stars and stories story.

characters.name: Piglet
characters.description: A diminutive and loyal companion, this small, thoughtful creature balances high-spirited play with Christopher Robin against a dedicated role in the group’s collaborative drawing sessions. Ever-attentive and earnest, he now channels his quiet focus into a heartfelt quest: discovering the perfect birthday gift for a cherished friend.

characters.name: Eeyore
characters.description: A somber donkey and companion to Tigger and Christopher Robin within the castle walls, he celebrates his birthday under a trademark cloud of gloom, certain that his friends have arrived empty-handed and forgotten his special day.

characters.name: Christopher Robin
characters.description: The master of the house at the final destination is a gentle boy and companion to Tigger, recently returned from a fishing trip to serve as the catalyst for the pilgrimage to the Wizard’s castle. Navigating the halls in a small car, he wanders the gallery of existence with a quiet, unsettling grace, pausing as a helpful friend to assist in inflating balloons. A reverent guest in his own domain, he visits Piglet—the foundational architect of a looming "big bad"—before setting the gears of the quest in motion and retiring home for a nap.

characters.name: Winnie the Pooh
characters.description: A hungry yet generous soul, this bear shares his honey and ladder with Hattie on their quest for the perfect golden harvest. By stilling his heart to hear the world’s quiet whispers, he has mastered the art of patient reflection, teaching his companions that even the greatest obstacles yield to the gentle power of waiting and thoughtful stillness.

places.name: Piglet's House
places.description: The place where Piglet keeps a packet of balloons of many different colors.

places.name: Christopher Robin's House
places.description: Nestled in a gentle clearing within the Hundred Acre Wood, this cozy house serves as a primary landing site for wind-tossed travelers. It is a place of simple wonders where Piglet often retreats, seeking a steady hand to help inflate his colorful balloons before they drift above the treetops.

places.name: Pooh Bear's House
places.description: The cozy residence of Winnie the Pooh, a sun-drenched haven where friends gather for indulgent feasts of savory cheese and golden honey, and where Pooh and Piglet sit in patient silence, waiting for the gentle arrival of a new idea.

places.name: The Stream
places.description: Tucked within the lush jungle near the mouth of Goofy’s tunnel, this refreshing basin offers a cool reprieve to any traveler patient enough to follow a local frog's lead. Its mirror-still surface serves as a somber sanctuary where Eeyore retreats on his birthday, spending the hours in quiet contemplation of his own reflection.
